title: Head of Production - EV Division, EMEA
department: BMW Group Production - EV Division EMEA
role_summary:
BMW Group is seeking an experienced production executive to lead EV production performance across the EMEA manufacturing network. The Head of Production - EV Division, EMEA will hold end-to-end accountability for operational execution, launch readiness, quality performance, workforce leadership, and continuous improvement across a strategically critical and high-visibility production environment. The role will operate across multiple Werk locations and will serve as the central production leadership counterpart to Quality, Supply Chain, Finance, HR Manufacturing, Industrial Engineering, and plant leadership teams.

A core responsibility of the role is to ensure that all relevant EV production units deliver against BMW Group requirements for safety, quality, cost, delivery, flexibility, and people performance within the framework of the BMW Production System (BPS). The successful candidate will lead production governance for serial operations while also supporting complex Start of Production (SOP) and ramp-up activities linked to Neue Klasse and other next-generation EV programs.

The position requires an executive capable of protecting operational stability while accelerating industrial transformation. This includes maintaining rigorous quality gate discipline, managing production risk in collaboration with Supply Chain and Quality, improving digital manufacturing capability, and ensuring readiness in a co-determined environment shaped by works council engagement and Mitbestimmung requirements. The role demands a strong blend of manufacturing depth, leadership at scale, stakeholder credibility, and the ability to translate strategic priorities into measurable production outcomes across the EMEA network.
key_responsibilities: Lead EV production performance across the EMEA manufacturing network in alignment with BMW Group production targets.
Ensure robust execution of the BMW Production System (BPS) across all relevant production units and plants.
Own production readiness, launch stability, and SOP execution for critical EV programs including Neue Klasse-related activities.
Drive achievement of safety, quality, cost, delivery, flexibility, and productivity targets across the regional production footprint.
Maintain disciplined quality gate governance in close partnership with Quality, Manufacturing Engineering, and Plant Management.
Provide executive oversight for capacity planning, throughput optimization, escalation management, and recovery planning during operational disruption.
Partner with Supply Chain and Purchasing to manage supply risk, material constraints, and production continuity challenges.
Lead, develop, and calibrate senior production leaders across the EMEA EV network.
Drive adoption of digital manufacturing and Industry 4.0 capabilities where they improve transparency, process control, and execution performance.
Manage budget performance and support investment decisions through fact-based business cases and clear ROI logic.
Represent Production in cross-functional executive governance with Finance, HR Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, and Strategy.
Ensure effective collaboration with works councils and employee representatives in the context of structural and operational change.
required_qualifications: University degree in engineering, manufacturing, industrial engineering, operations management, or a related technical discipline.
Significant senior leadership experience in automotive or advanced manufacturing production environments.
Proven track record in EV or complex vehicle production management within a multi-plant or regional scope.
Demonstrated experience leading SOP, ramp-up, or industrialization programs in high-complexity manufacturing settings.
Deep knowledge of lean production systems and structured operational excellence frameworks such as BPS-equivalent environments.
Strong quality management experience, including quality gates, process capability discipline, and automotive quality systems.
Experience leading large-scale production organizations and senior managers in matrixed and co-determined environments.
Strong cross-functional stakeholder management capability across Quality, Supply Chain, HR, Finance, and Engineering.
Demonstrated financial acumen including budget ownership and translation of operational decisions into business impact.
Fluent English and strong German sufficient for executive and works council environments.
preferred_qualifications: Direct experience with Neue Klasse-related technologies, EV platform industrialization, or next-generation battery and e-drive manufacturing.
Experience in digital manufacturing, Industry 4.0, MES, analytics-enabled production control, or smart factory transformation.
Track record of leading production organizations through significant change while maintaining operational stability.
Experience managing supplier-related disruption, launch recovery, or major production crises in the automotive sector.
International leadership experience across the EMEA manufacturing footprint.
Advanced qualification such as MBA, doctorate, or executive manufacturing leadership program.
leadership_competencies: Demonstrates BMW-aligned leadership by combining operational discipline with transformation capability.
Builds trust and execution alignment across functions, plants, and stakeholder groups in a complex manufacturing environment.
Leads through clarity, accountability, and fact-based decision-making while respecting Mitbestimmung and organizational context.
Drives sustainable performance improvement through structured problem-solving and disciplined escalation.
Develops leaders and strengthens workforce capability in support of long-term production excellence.
Translates strategic priorities into executable production outcomes with measurable business impact.

scope_and_scale.direct_reports: 6-9 senior production and plant leadership reports
scope_and_scale.total_workforce: Approx. 8,000-15,000 employees across direct and indirect production scope
scope_and_scale.annual_budget: Approx. EUR 1.2-2.0 billion operating budget influence across the production network
scope_and_scale.plants_overseen: Multiple EV-relevant production sites and associated regional manufacturing interfaces across EMEA
scope_and_scale.key_programs: Neue Klasse industrialization, EV SOP readiness, quality stabilization, BPS maturity, digital manufacturing enablement, workforce capability development

context_notes: The role is open due to a planned leadership transition at a strategically sensitive point for BMW Group Production. Immediate priorities include safeguarding SOP readiness, improving execution consistency across the network, strengthening collaboration with Quality and Supply Chain, and increasing the organization's ability to absorb digital and structural change without destabilizing core production performance.
